source: Curated from quantumalgorithmzoo.org + Troyer framework analysis

algorithms.name: Shor's Algorithm
algorithms.notes: The canonical example. Breaks RSA. Needs ~4000 logical qubits for RSA-2048.

algorithms.name: Quantum Phase Estimation (QPE)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for naturally quantum Hamiltonians. Core of quantum chemistry advantage.

algorithms.name: Hamiltonian Simulation (Product Formulas)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for simulating quantum systems. Feynman's original motivation.

algorithms.name: Quantum Walk (Transport)
algorithms.classical_best: Lindblad master equation (classical simulation of open quantum systems)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for naturally quantum transport. Avoids I/O problem.

algorithms.name: Lattice Gauge Theory (Real-Time)
algorithms.classical_best: Lattice QCD (Euclidean only, sign problem for real-time)
algorithms.notes: Classical lattice QCD cannot do real-time dynamics (sign problem). Strongest long-term utility case.

algorithms.name: Grover's Search
algorithms.notes: Provably optimal quadratic speedup but QRAM loading O(N) erases it. Oracle cost for real problems (AES, database) dominates.

algorithms.name: Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE)
algorithms.notes: Quadratic speedup proven. But QEC overhead requires ε < 10⁻⁴ for net advantage. Distribution loading is O(N).

algorithms.name: HHL Algorithm
algorithms.notes: Exponential core speedup but O(N) state prep and O(N) readout on both ends. Only useful if you need the quantum state, not the classical solution vector.

algorithms.name: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE)
algorithms.notes: No proven speedup. Barren plateaus at scale. Classical tensor networks often competitive. Use QPE instead for fault-tolerant era.

algorithms.name: QAOA (Quantum Approximate Optimization)
algorithms.notes: No proven advantage beyond quadratic. Constant-depth QAOA is classically simulable. GW achieves 0.878-approx for MaxCut in poly time.

algorithms.name: Quantum Machine Learning (Kernel Methods)
algorithms.notes: Exponential kernel speedup exists but O(N) data loading negates it. Proven advantages only for specific contrived problems.

algorithms.name: Quantum Error Correction (Surface Code)
algorithms.quantum_complexity: O(d²) physical qubits per logical qubit
algorithms.notes: Not an algorithm — enabling technology. Threshold theorem guarantees fault-tolerant computation if physical error rate < threshold.
